4 /** 5 * A [Client Component](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/rendering/client-components) hook 6 * that lets you *read* the current URL's search parameters. 7 * 8 * Learn more about [`URLSearchParams` on MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/URLSearchParams) 9 * 10 * @example 11 * ```ts 12 * "use client" 13 * import { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation' 14 * 15 * export default function Page() { 16 * const searchParams = useSearchParams() 17 * searchParams.get('foo') // returns 'bar' when ?foo=bar 18 * // ... 19 * } 20 * ``` 21 * 22 * Read more: [Next.js Docs: `useSearchParams`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-search-params) 23 */ 24 export declare function useSearchParams(): ReadonlyURLSearchParams; 25 /** 26 * A [Client Component](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/rendering/client-components) hook 27 * that lets you read the current URL's pathname. 28 * 29 * @example 30 * ```ts 31 * "use client" 32 * import { usePathname } from 'next/navigation' 33 * 34 * export default function Page() { 35 * const pathname = usePathname() // returns "/dashboard" on /dashboard?foo=bar 36 * // ... 37 * } 38 * ``` 39 * 40 * Read more: [Next.js Docs: `usePathname`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-pathname) 41 */ 42 export declare function usePathname(): string;
